sglang.title: Fix deepseek-v3 with torch.compile in PyTorch 2.6.
sglang.author: zou3519
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
In PyTorch 2.6, we erroneously changed the behavior of torch.compile to coerce all inputs to custom operators to be contiguous. This is wrong when the custom operator is expecting a non-contiguous Tensor.
In PyTorch 2.7 we have since fixed that. This PR is a workaround for PyTorch 2.6.
Mo

sglang.title: Fix torch.compile cacheing
sglang.author: zou3519
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
torch.compile caching does not work well with sglang.
Modifications
The problem was that torch.compile thinks part of its internals is not cacheable. torch.compile is wrong. We'll fix this on the PyTorch side for PyTorch 2.8+, but in the meantime, it's pretty easy to tell torch.compile th

sglang.title: ROCm/AITER CK_MoE: update 2-stage kernels & support both Activations
sglang.author: HaiShaw
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

Performance boost from AITER Fused MoE, 2-stage kernels
Support:
SiLU:   bfloat16, float8, INT4-FP8, models: Mixtral, Command R, DeepSeek, etc.
GeLU:  bfloat16, float8, INT4-FP8, models: Grok
Modifications
GeLU + block quant is yet supported

vllm.title: [Kernel] Use moe_wna16 kernel for compressed tensors wna16 moe models
vllm.author: mgoin
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Similar to #13236, but now to bring the moe_wna16 kernel to models in compressed-tensors format. This is crucial for models with many experts to have good performance or require bfloat16 dtype. We should still move to using the quantization/kernel/ interface to implement this kernel selection proper

python3 examples/offline_inference/vision_language_multi_image.py --model_type llama4

Summary: In fbcode, importlib.metadata.version doesn't work (no PKG-INFO), so we need to switch to a compatible way to load versions.
Reviewed By: henryoier
Differential Revision: D72221498

vllm.title: [VLM] Remove `BaseProcessingInfo.get_mm_max_tokens_per_item`
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
It is somewhat redundant to use BaseProcessingInfo.get_mm_max_tokens_per_item when we can directly get the number of multi-modal tokens from the dummy data now (via PlaceholderRange.get_num_embeds added by #15712).
Although this means we need to generate the dummy data to get the multi-modal token c

vllm.title: [TPU][V1] Use `language_model` interface for getting text backbone in MM
vllm.author: NickLucche
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Follow up on #15782.
Use the SupportsMultiModal.get_language_model method now that it is available.

vllm.title: Improve configs - `ParallelConfig`
vllm.author: hmellor
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
As suggested in #15724 (review)
This PR picks an arbitrary config class and sets the design that the others should adopt.
Other configs will be changed in follow up PRs to make reviewing simpler.
Key principles:

Default values and documentation belongs to the config dataclass
EngineArgs (and its ar

vllm.title: [V1] Set structured output backend to `auto` by default
vllm.author: russellb
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
The previous default was xgrammar and users had to opt-in to fallback
behavior. After more thought, auto seems like a better default as it
lets us do our best to satisfy all requests. Users can still pin vllm to
a single backend if desired.
Signed-off-by: Russell Bryant rbryant@redhat.com

Task 1 of #15901
Some limitations:

only tested with single GPU
only works with eager mode for both target and draft models, need to check compatibility with torch compile
only support llama models

How to run this PR:
python examples/offline_inference/eagle.py

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix bug when dataset is json
vllm.author: Chenyaaang
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
In benchmark_serving_structured_output.py, when dataset is json, use the original loaded schema instead of creating json_schemas.
The sample command provided in benchmark_serving_structured_output.py doesn't work because it fails at sample_requests() for json dataset.
python benchmarks/benchmark_ser

vllm.title: [Model] Reduce redundant computations in mamba2 blocks for Bamba-9B
vllm.author: cyang49
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Observed redundant computations for common metadata across mamba2 layers. This PR attempts to reduce them as much as possible. It will be done incrementally through several smaller commits.
Original: 1.5ms redundant computations per mamba2 layer

With this PR: single 1.5ms computation per model forw

vllm.title: [V1] Zero-copy tensor/ndarray serialization/transmission
vllm.author: njhill
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Update the custom msgpack encoding/decoding to work with lists of buffers so that the backing data of tensors/numpy arrays contained in messages is sent directly by zmq without copying.
This is a first step, we still need to update the message schemas that we are using to exploit this. In particular

vllm.title: [VLM] Avoid unnecessary dummy multimodal data during processing
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Currently with the cache enabled, in BaseMultiModalProcessor._apply_hf_processor_mm_only we create both text and multimodal dummy data but the latter is unused, leading to unnecessary overhead for allocating NumPy arrays. This is especially devastating for models that support large image sizes such

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix output token length check logic
vllm.author: eeslook
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
When the model interface doesn't return usage, output_tokens value defaults to 0
Using 'is None' check fails to correctly calculate output_len in this case

This fix ensures proper calculation of output length even when usage data is not provided.

vllm.title: Fix range_ratio Bug in RandomDataset
vllm.author: jadewang21
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Description
This PR fixes a bug in the sample method of the RandomDataset class. When range_ratio > 1, the calculation of input_low and output_low would result in low >= high, causing a ValueError: low >= high.
Issue

In the original code, input_low = int(input_len * range_ratio) and input_high = in

vllm.title: check input length of sonnet samples
vllm.author: alexey-belyakov
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
FIX #16420
This PR adds a check for [prompt_len] before including it in the samples.
In the scenario described in the related issue, approximately 4% of requests failed due to input length limitations. This issue is influenced by the tokenizer and randomness in the selection process.
The root cause

vllm.author: Chenyaaang
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
follow up #15724
After setting structured output backend to auto, update the benchmark script to also accept "auto" as structured-output-backend.

vllm.title: [Llama4] Enable attention temperature tuning by default for long context (>32k)
vllm.author: shinoss
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Attention temperature tuning (on nope layers) improves accuracy on long context (>32k) tasks. Enabling it by default for long context unless explicitly disabled by the user.
Eval results on RULER with and without attention temperature tuning (@luccafong):

cc: @ywang96 @simon-mo @DarkLight1337

vllm.title: [Bugfix][VLM] Fix failing Phi-4-MM multi-images tests and add vision-speech test
vllm.author: Isotr0py
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Separate from #15477 in case of potential regression issue.
Fix Phi-4-mm divergence issues due to ineffective LoRA enablement. Because llm.llm_engine.add_lora(lora_request=lora_request) didn't enable LoRA during inference exactly.

Also cc @jeejeelee in case there is a bug at LoRA side.

Not sure why this isn't allowed at the moment, maybe because of validation for allowed ranges for frequency/presence penalties based on openai doc
